sections.Faction Overview:
Public FaceThe BCI presents itself as Meridian Spire’s guardian against cognitive contamination, trauma spirals, dangerous memory instability, and illegal memory technology.
Actual TruthThe BCI protects the edited version of history. It uses surveillance, records manipulation, medicalized memory correction, propaganda, and tactical force to keep the city stable and obedient.
Player-Safe ReadCitizens know the BCI as stern but necessary. Licensed PIs know the BCI can freeze cases, revoke access, lock terminals, and make evidence disappear.
GM WarningThe BCI should feel lawful before it feels evil. Their scariest power is not violence; it is making everyone agree the violence was necessary.
TerritoryCivic Core, Cognitive Health Clinics, BCI Processing Towers, patrol zones, black-site annexes, and emergency response corridors.
white BCI letteringred enforcement lightsgreen terminal scansclean geometric armorofficial seals
sections.Internal Structure:
DirectoratePolicy, citywide control, elite coordination, classified continuity orders.
Internal SecurityLeak suppression, erased case files, black-bag warrants, evidence laundering.
Patrol DivisionStreet presence, checkpoint authority, PI interference, public compliance.
Enforcer CorpsHigh-risk tactical response, raids, forced extractions, memory seizure operations.
Cognitive Health DivisionTherapeutic memory correction, compliance medicine, post-incident reconditioning.
Public Trust OfficePropaganda, citizen advisories, controlled crisis narratives.
sections.Director Mara Veyne:
Director of the BCI
“Your memories are protected. Your fear is understood. Your cooperation is required.”
Public: Trusted public face of cognitive safety. Appears on citywide broadcasts after crises.
Truth: Knows memory alteration has been used politically and believes the lie is necessary to preserve Meridian Spire.
Open full dossier
Vibe: Cold, elegant, terrifyingly calm.
Secret: Has seen original massacre records and the edits that followed.
First Appearance: Broadcasts, propaganda screens, official warnings, and terminal overrides.
DM Use: Use when the party becomes a city-level problem.
CO
BCI
sections.Captain Orin Vale:
BCI Patrol Captain
“Licensed private investigators. Interesting. That makes this unfortunate instead of suspicious.”
Public: Field captain assigned to disturbances, suspicious PI activity, and illegal information cases.
Truth: Ambitious, observant, and willing to use the party as a stepping stone or case file.
Open full dossier
Vibe: Professional, sharp, irritatingly reasonable.
Secret: Suspects BCI hides something larger than routine corruption but lacks courage to pursue it.
First Appearance: Arrives after the Session 1 apartment incident or after a terminal hack alert.
DM Use: Early recurring antagonist. Threatens licenses, scenes, witnesses, and access.
DK
BCI
sections.Commander Darius Korr:
BCI Enforcer Commander
“End this now. Compliance is your last mercy.”
Public: Male commander of high-risk BCI tactical response teams. Known mostly through red-alert footage.
Truth: Leads black-bag raids, memory seizure operations, forced disappearances, and suppression missions.
Open full dossier
Vibe: Tactical hammer of the BCI. Minimal words, maximum pressure.
Secret: Remembers every major raid, especially the ones involving innocents. He buries guilt under obedience.
First Appearance: Red emergency lights, synchronized Enforcer units, no negotiation posture.
DM Use: Use sparingly as a major escalation. His arrival means survival mode.
DH
BCI
sections.Deputy Director Halden Kreiss:
Internal Security Chief
“A disappeared file is not evidence. It is an administrative correction.”
Public: Senior BCI administrator responsible for internal security and classified investigations.
Truth: Handles leaks, coverups, disappearances, and political damage control.
Open full dossier
Vibe: Bureaucratic monster; can ruin lives with paperwork.
Secret: Approved cleanup operations tied to family disappearance cases, including Cross and Ruiz threads.
First Appearance: An authorization stamp on a redacted report.
DM Use: Use when players trace who signed off on ruined lives.
ES
BCI
sections.Dr. Elian Strake:
Memory Compliance Doctor
“The mind protects itself. We simply assist the process.”
Public: Respected cognitive health specialist involved in trauma treatment and memory stabilization.
Truth: Performs therapeutic memory correction that hides abuse, erases people, and reframes history.
Open full dossier
Vibe: Gentle voice, clean gloves, absolute nightmare.
Secret: Has treated people who later forgot entire family members.
First Appearance: Interviewing a victim who suddenly cannot remember why they were afraid.
DM Use: Make memory editing intimate, plausible, and horrifying.
CV
BCI
sections.Celia Voss:
Propaganda Minister
“A frightened public is not unstable. It is receptive.”
Public: Media strategist behind BCI safety campaigns and anti-rebel broadcasts.
Truth: Controls the language people use to fear illegal information, rebels, and memory contamination.
Open full dossier
Vibe: Perfect smile, poisonous words.
Secret: No longer fully believes the propaganda, but is too deep to stop.
First Appearance: A broadcast reframes the party’s investigation as a public safety concern.
DM Use: Controls public reputation and narrative consequences.
sections.Session Hooks:
• BCI freezes the party’s PI licenses after the Damien Ruiz scene.
• A witness forgets key details after a “wellness intervention.”
• Korr’s Enforcers arrive before the party can copy a terminal file.
• Celia Voss turns the party into dangerous conspiracy agitators on public screens.
GM Secret Board
sections.Faction Secrets:
• The BCI did not create every lie, but it actively maintains the largest ones.
• Internal Security has a cleanup classification for family-disappearance cases.
• Some BCI doctors believe they are reducing trauma, not committing violence.
• The original massacre records still exist in heavily protected fragments.

sections.Faction Overview:
Public FaceThe BCI describes the Choir as dangerous agitators who radicalize the grieving and spread illegal memory material.
Actual TruthThe Choir believes forgotten people have been murdered twice: once in life and once in memory. They fight to recover names, stories, and proof of erased lives.
Player-Safe ReadCitizens know graffiti, hacked screens, and whispers: “The Choir remembers.” PIs hear that they can find witnesses nobody else can.
GM WarningThe Choir should not be clean heroes. They are right about the crime, but desperation can make them reckless and dangerous.
TerritoryThe Dregs, runoff tunnels, slum sectors, pirate relay stations, hidden memorial rooms, abandoned civic shelters.
painted eyesbroken halospirate radio staticundercity muralsnames written over names
sections.Internal Structure:
The Mother’s CircleSenior voices around Aven Sorell; strategy, doctrine, grief rituals.
Runoff CellsLocal crews that hide people, move shards, and run street actions.
Signal ChoirHackers, artists, pirate broadcasters, propaganda makers.
Witness KeepersMembers who protect people with unstable or returning memories.
Remembrance RitesPublic mourning actions that double as protests and coded messages.
sections.Mother Aven Sorell:
Leader of the Choir
“They taught you memory is fragile. They never told you who keeps breaking it.”
Public: Classified as a radical agitator, cult leader, and domestic extremist.
Truth: Former BCI grief specialist who learned therapeutic memory practices were being weaponized.
Open full dossier
Vibe: Revolutionary grief-preacher; compassionate and dangerous.
Secret: Helped shape early therapeutic memory correction before seeing what the system became.
First Appearance: A hacked public terminal interrupts a BCI broadcast with her voice.
DM Use: Make her right about the horror but questionable in what she will sacrifice.
JV
CotU
sections.Jessa Vane:
Recruiter and Propagandist
“You want the truth? Then stop asking politely.”
Public: Young dissident blamed for illegal data pages and anti-BCI recruitment messages.
Truth: Builds contact chains, designs rebellious data drops, and may have helped Damien arrange the failed exchange.
Open full dossier
Vibe: Angry, brilliant, reckless hacker-recruiter.
Secret: Knew Damien personally and is hiding how much she knows about the meet.
First Appearance: A rebellious data page asks if the viewer wants to know the truth.
DM Use: Hot fuse: useful, emotional, likely to escalate problems.
BS
CotU
sections.Bram “Static” Olan:
Runoff Cell Leader
“The city whispers down here. You just have to hate silence enough to listen.”
Public: Rumored undercity agitator linked to illegal broadcasts and tunnel activity.
Truth: Runs CotU movement in the runoff and suspects Damien was betrayed from inside the Choir.
Open full dossier
Vibe: Pirate radio preacher with a shotgun personality.
Secret: Believes there is a mole inside his cell.
First Appearance: His voice crackles through a hacked runoff speaker before the party sees him.
DM Use: Ground-level resistance, ugly choices, tunnel politics.
TH
CotU
sections.The Hollow Man:
Memory Witness
“I remember three childhoods. Only one of them has my mother in it.”
Public: Used by BCI propaganda as proof illegal memory material causes psychosis.
Truth: His edited memories are breaking through in incompatible versions.
Open full dossier
Vibe: Creepy, tragic, glitching humanity.
Secret: One memory fragment contains a real name erased from city history.
First Appearance: Found repeating the same address in three different voices.
DM Use: Disturbing clues, sympathy, impossible testimony.
SL
CotU
sections.Saint Luma:
Martyr Symbol
“Remember Luma. Remember what they buried.”
Public: Supposed propaganda invention used by CotU to manipulate the desperate.
Truth: Luma was real, erased, and turned into a symbol by those who refused to forget.
Open full dossier
Vibe: Urban saint, missing-person poster, ghost in the movement.
Secret: The true record of Luma’s death points toward the original massacre.
First Appearance: A mural in the Dregs, painted over so many times it became holy.
DM Use: Clue chain and emotional center for the Choir.
sections.Session Hooks:
• A CotU data page asks the players if they want to know the truth.
• Bram believes someone inside the Choir sold out Damien.
• A remembrance mural contains a hidden address.
• The Hollow Man remembers a version of a PC that should not exist.
GM Secret Board
sections.Faction Secrets:
• Aven was part of the system before becoming its enemy.
• The Choir’s younger cells are less disciplined and may hurt innocents.
• Saint Luma’s erased death links to the original massacre.
• Someone inside a runoff cell may be feeding BCI information.

sections.Faction Overview:
Public FaceMost citizens do not know Truth Division exists. BCI files describe them as conspiracy terrorists spreading corrosive historical falsehoods.
Actual TruthTruth Division wants the unedited past exposed, even if the revelation destabilizes Meridian Spire. They are less emotional than CotU and less patient than the Red Archive.
Player-Safe ReadPlayers first experience them through purple flashes, terminal warnings, and messages like “you aren’t ready.”
GM WarningTruth Division should be allies who make the party nervous. They value truth more than comfort, safety, or timing.
TerritoryHidden servers, dead terminals, ghost forums, compromised civic relays, old academic networks, abandoned data vaults.
purple signal flashesfragmented timelinesquestion marks cut into sealsterminal intrusionsbroken archives
sections.Internal Structure:
Directive CellSmall leadership group deciding which truths can be released.
Signal CuttersHackers who breach terminals, relays, and surveillance systems.
Witness ExtractorsField teams who remove targets before BCI can edit them.
Timeline AnalystsResearchers who compare contradictory records.
Radical SplintersCells that want a full truth-dump regardless of casualties.
sections.Director Ilyra Noct:
Leader of Truth Division
“Truth is not gentle. That is not an argument against it.”
Public: Unknown or misidentified as a fringe conspiracy coordinator.
Truth: Leads Truth Division and believes the city must see the real past no matter the damage.
Open full dossier
Vibe: Cold revolutionary scholar with a knife behind every sentence.
Secret: Has seen a partial unedited timeline and it broke her faith in gradual reform.
First Appearance: A purple terminal message signs off with only NOCT.
DM Use: Use when the party needs someone who values truth more than safety.
LS
Truth Division
sections.Lio Senn:
Truth Division Hacker
“You weren’t hacked. You were invited badly.”
Public: No official record. Known through purple glitches and impossible terminal intrusions.
Truth: Responsible for Truth Division hacks, warnings, and distorted terminal messages.
Open full dossier
Vibe: Sleep-deprived genius gremlin with a moral injury.
Secret: Knows Ash’s name appears in files that should not exist.
First Appearance: A purple flash hijacks a terminal and leaves a line only the party understands.
DM Use: Scary terminal scenes, encrypted clues, unreliable assistance.
NC
Truth Division
sections.Nessa Crowe:
Ex-BCI Defector
“I know how they hunt you because I used to write the hunt orders.”
Public: Officially dead, disgraced, or nonexistent depending on the database.
Truth: Former BCI operative with knowledge of tracking, editing, and threat classification.
Open full dossier
Vibe: Burned spy; paranoid, deadly, hard to trust.
Secret: Helped erase someone once and has tried to undo it ever since.
First Appearance: Saves the party from a BCI trace, then aims a weapon at them.
DM Use: Teach BCI countermeasures and make trust expensive.
TW
Truth Division
sections.Tomas Wren:
Field Archivist
“I don’t collect evidence. I collect the parts they forgot to erase.”
Public: Listed as an antiquarian data thief.
Truth: Collects old evidence, corrupted files, and erased identities for Truth Division.
Open full dossier
Vibe: Tired field scholar with too many dead drops.
Secret: Carries a physical notebook of names that vanished from digital systems.
First Appearance: Found photographing moldy paper records before BCI scanners arrive.
DM Use: Gives physical clues and old-world investigative texture.
KM
Truth Division
sections.Kade Morrow:
Radical Truth Cell Leader
“You can’t expose a prison gently. You break the wall.”
Public: Violent dissident wanted for data terrorism.
Truth: Wants immediate mass release of unedited history, consequences be damned.
Open full dossier
Vibe: Charismatic, furious, dangerous even when correct.
Secret: Has a release package that could ignite riots across several sectors.
First Appearance: Offers the party the truth faster than Noct will.
DM Use: Temptation NPC: easy answers, catastrophic cost.
V
Truth Division
sections.Violet:
Ash Calder Contact
“You aren’t ready. That is not mercy. It is math.”
Public: Unknown purple-signal alias.
Truth: A Truth Division contact monitoring Ash and the transfer-related files around him.
Open full dossier
Vibe: Ghost in the terminal, intimate and unsettling.
Secret: May know what Ash is before Ash does.
First Appearance: Appears as a purple flash when Ash opens the wrong file.
DM Use: Personalized pressure on Ash’s mystery.
sections.Session Hooks:
• A terminal flashes purple and locks the party out after showing Ash’s name.
• Nessa Crowe offers BCI counter-surveillance in exchange for a dangerous favor.
• Kade Morrow offers a complete leak package that will cause riots.
• Noct demands the party prove they can handle truth before receiving the next file.
GM Secret Board
sections.Faction Secrets:
• Truth Division may be holding back truth just like the groups they condemn.
• Lio knows the party is connected to files older than their public records.
• A radical cell wants to release everything at once.
• Noct’s partial timeline includes a name linked to one of the PCs.

sections.Faction Overview:
Public FaceOfficially, the Red Archive does not exist. Unofficially, people whisper that someone kept the records the city tried to bury.
Actual TruthThe Red Archive preserves forbidden records, erased names, and pre-edited history. They believe truth is a weapon and should not be handed to everyone.
Player-Safe ReadPlayers may first encounter the Archive through a refined field agent, impossible document access, or a red mark left at a scene.
GM WarningThe Archive is not heroic. They protect truth by controlling it. They may help the party while still manipulating them.
TerritoryHidden archives, locked museums, abandoned libraries, private vaults, gallery fronts, data mausoleums.
redacted recordscrimson sealswhite glovesold paper scansthin red thread
sections.Internal Structure:
The ArchivistCassian Rell and the final authority on what is released.
Field RetrievalAgents who steal, rescue, or destroy sensitive records.
Historical AnalysisResearchers who compare old records against edited timelines.
Memory CurationSpecialists in damaged memories and transfer-adjacent evidence.
CleanersQuiet removers of threats to Archive secrecy.
BrokersPeople who trade controlled truths for leverage.
sections.Archivist Cassian Rell:
Head of the Red Archive
“The truth is not sacred. It is dangerous. That is why it must be kept properly.”
Public: Unknown. Officially nonexistent.
Truth: Keeper of original records, erased names, and pre-edited history fragments.
Open full dossier
Vibe: Refined, quiet, deeply unsettling.
Secret: Possesses names of people completely erased from official history.
First Appearance: Through an intermediary; he should be a later reveal.
DM Use: Gatekeeper of dangerous truths. The party must earn what he knows.
AS
Red Archive
sections.Agent Seraphine Vale:
Field Agent
“I am not here to save you. I am here to preserve what you found.”
Public: No public record that survives more than a day.
Truth: Elegant operative who retrieves forbidden records and evaluates whether witnesses are assets or liabilities.
Open full dossier
Vibe: Sophisticated, minimalistic armor, quiet authority.
Secret: Has watched the party since the Damien Ruiz incident.
First Appearance: Appears after the party uncovers evidence nobody else should know exists.
DM Use: First direct contact with the Red Archive.
LO
Red Archive
sections.Len Orra:
Historical Analyst
“Clean records are suspicious. Real history leaves fingerprints.”
Public: Obscure researcher with missing credentials.
Truth: Studies erased timelines, rewritten dates, and altered public records.
Open full dossier
Vibe: Nervous academic who knows way too much.
Secret: Found proof that official city events were rewritten multiple times.
First Appearance: Explains why a perfectly clean record is the dirtiest clue in the room.
DM Use: Translate record weirdness into usable leads.
VS
Red Archive
sections.Dr. Veyra Sol:
Memory Curator
“A broken memory is not useless. It is evidence with teeth marks.”
Public: Former cognitive researcher absent from official boards.
Truth: Studies damaged memories, identity records, and transfer-adjacent anomalies.
Open full dossier
Vibe: Precise, compassionate, unnerving.
Secret: Has a private theory connecting Ash, ECHO-7, and the first transfer trials.
First Appearance: Examines a corrupted memory shard and refuses to say everything she saw.
DM Use: Bridge Red Archive research with memory horror.
ER
Red Archive
sections.Enoch Rime:
Cleaner
“Some records survive better when witnesses do not.”
Public: None.
Truth: Removes people and evidence that threaten the Archive’s secrecy.
Open full dossier
Vibe: Silent assassin with funeral manners.
Secret: Does not always agree with Cassian’s orders.
First Appearance: Already in the room when the party arrives.
DM Use: A warning that the Archive can be just as frightening as BCI.
MG
Red Archive
sections.Mr. Glass:
Archive Broker
“Information does not want to be free. Information wants better clients.”
Public: Private consultant to wealthy collectors and officials.
Truth: Trades secrets with politicians, criminals, and corporations while shielding the Archive.
Open full dossier
Vibe: Charming, predatory, immaculate.
Secret: Has sold truths that got people killed.
First Appearance: Offers the party a record, but only if they trade something personal.
DM Use: Make information feel expensive and morally loaded.
sections.Session Hooks:
• Seraphine saves a record from destruction, not necessarily the people holding it.
• Cassian offers one truth in exchange for the party burying another.
• Len proves Damien’s file was rewritten by comparing imperfect paper records.
• Enoch Rime appears when the party leaks something too soon.
GM Secret Board
sections.Faction Secrets:
• The Archive has records the BCI believes were destroyed.
• Cassian may know the real name behind Saint Luma.
• The Archive has already classified the PCs as either assets or risks.
• Some Archive brokers are profiting from the exact system the Archive claims to resist.

sections.Faction Overview:
Public FaceContinuance claims to treat neurological decay, trauma loss, identity collapse, and memory deterioration through advanced continuity research.
Actual TruthContinuance is one of the few groups that knows memory transfer is real. Its leadership is obsessed with preserving identity beyond death, especially for the powerful.
Player-Safe ReadTo the public, Continuance sounds hopeful and humane. To people who dig too deep, it feels like death wearing a lab coat.
GM WarningContinuance should make the party ask whether survival without the original body is mercy, theft, or a copy pretending to be a person.
TerritoryCorporate labs, private hospitals, sealed research floors, donor clinics, prototype containment, elite gala spaces.
glass labsblue-white lightsynthetic bodiescontinuity ringsmedical branding
sections.Internal Structure:
Founding BoardWealth, political sponsorship, and strategic protection.
Continuity ResearchMemory transfer, identity mapping, synthetic housing, neurological backups.
Ethics OfficePublic reassurance, consent frameworks, legal insulation.
Containment SecurityRetrieval of subjects, witness control, prototype protection.
Donor ProgramUltra-rich patrons funding immortality-adjacent research.
sections.Dr. Adrian Kest:
Founder
“Death is only failure with better branding.”
Public: Billionaire scientist and philanthropist trying to cure memory loss and identity collapse.
Truth: Obsessed with defeating death through continuity of self, not simply healing people.
Open full dossier
Vibe: Tech prophet with something dead behind the eyes.
Secret: Knows of, funded, or directly oversaw projects related to Ash’s condition.
First Appearance: Public gala, news broadcast, high-security lab, or Ash-linked records.
DM Use: Central SOMA-style dilemma NPC.
MC
Continuance
sections.Dr. Mira Cale:
Lead Memory Transfer Scientist
“No... that can’t be right. You shouldn’t exist like this.”
Public: Neural continuity researcher known mostly in technical circles.
Truth: Worked directly on memory transfer and can explain what Ash really is.
Open full dossier
Vibe: Brilliant, exhausted, guilty.
Secret: Remembers the first successful transfer, and it was not peaceful.
First Appearance: Recognizes Ash before he introduces himself.
DM Use: Emotional and scientific doorway into Ash’s secret.
E
Continuance
sections.ECHO-7:
Continuance Prototype
“I remember dying. I do not remember stopping.”
Public: No public existence.
Truth: Artificial body or continuity vessel containing transferred memory data.
Open full dossier
Vibe: Tragic, uncanny, childlike and ancient by turns.
Secret: May remember events from before its body existed.
First Appearance: Hidden, escaped, or contained in a quiet lab room that should be empty.
DM Use: Proof that memory transfer is real before the party fully understands Ash.
SM
Continuance
sections.Selene Marr:
Ethics Director
“The question is not whether we should continue. It is who gets to decide what counts as life.”
Public: Public-facing conscience of Continuance.
Truth: Makes horrifying research sound humane and legally survivable.
Open full dossier
Vibe: Soft-spoken corporate guilt in a tailored suit.
Secret: Tried to stop Kest before and failed.
First Appearance: Appears in a polished interview defending Continuance after rumors spread.
DM Use: Moral ambiguity: complicit but maybe redeemable.
GL
Continuance
sections.Garran Locke:
Security Director
“Recovery is not optional. Neither is silence.”
Public: Corporate protection executive.
Truth: Retrieves escaped subjects, silences witnesses, and protects labs.
Open full dossier
Vibe: Private military commander with corporate polish.
Secret: Has orders to recover Ash alive if the truth surfaces.
First Appearance: Clean SUVs and quiet armed professionals instead of street-level thugs.
DM Use: Threat when Ash’s existence attracts Continuance attention.
RT
Continuance
sections.Commander Rusk Thorne:
Political Donor / Security Hardliner
“Progress belongs to those with the courage to fund it.”
Public: Wealthy sponsor of security expansion and Continuance research.
Truth: Funds Continuance because he is dying and wants continuity for himself.
Open full dossier
Vibe: Old-money authoritarian terrified of mortality.
Secret: His donor file includes an emergency transfer priority.
First Appearance: Appears at a gala or city hearing arguing for BCI and Continuance partnership.
DM Use: Tie money, politics, security, and immortality together.
sections.Session Hooks:
• Mira recognizes Ash and panics.
• Garran Locke’s team tries to recover a person, not kill them.
• ECHO-7 begs the party not to let Continuance “continue” anyone else.
• Kest offers a beautiful explanation of immortality that sounds almost reasonable.
GM Secret Board
sections.Faction Secrets:
• Memory transfer is real but extremely rare and unstable.
• The first successful transfer may have suffered.
• Continuance keeps donor priority lists for powerful people.
• Ash’s existence may be evidence, property, miracle, or crime depending on who controls the story.

sections.Faction Overview:
Public FaceThe Grey Market is not one organization; it is the illegal economy of secrets. RedWire is one of the more feared cartels inside that economy.
Actual TruthInformation is currency in Meridian Spire. Grey Market brokers buy, steal, launder, and sell data, memory shards, identities, blackmail, surveillance, and BCI tech.
Player-Safe ReadThe party will need the Grey Market when official channels fail. The cost may be money, favors, reputation, or old secrets.
GM WarningThis faction works best when it is useful and awful at the same time. Let players like these NPCs, then remind them everyone has a price.
TerritoryBack rooms, nightclubs, market dens, monorail maintenance tunnels, runoff routes, private clinics, abandoned data shops.
red wiresealed envelopescash chipsblack-market terminalsmaintenance tunnels
sections.Internal Structure:
BrokersDeal secrets, identities, memory shards, and leverage.
CartelsRedWire and similar crews that control territory and enforce debts.
FixersConnect clients with smugglers, doctors, hackers, and fences.
Tunnel OperatorsMove people and goods through transit infrastructure.
Black ClinicsPatch wounds, remove trackers, alter records, no questions unless paid.
CollectorsDebt enforcers and reputation managers.
sections.Varo Kline:
Grey Market Kingpin
“Memory shard? No, no, no. I don’t touch those. Unless you have money. Then I become very brave.”
Public: Club owner, fixer, businessman, information logistics consultant.
Truth: Deals blackmail, stolen BCI tech, surveillance, memory shard movement, fake identities, and dangerous secrets.
Open full dossier
Vibe: Charming criminal uncle; expensive coat, cheap morals.
Secret: May already know Mozzie or the party by reputation.
First Appearance: Backroom beneath a club where music is louder than surveillance equipment.
DM Use: Recurring underworld contact: helpful, dangerous, never free.
SV
Grey Market / RedWire
sections.Silas Varn:
RedWire Cartel Boss
“Nobody leaves clean. Not even the ones I let walk.”
Public: Unknown crime figure.
Truth: Runs RedWire operations in the Dregs and lower Meridian Spire. Allowed Colton to leave alive.
Open full dossier
Vibe: Old-school crime boss with cyberpunk paranoia.
Secret: Knows Colton was Iron Cross.
First Appearance: Appears when RedWire decides Colton’s detective life has become inconvenient.
DM Use: The scariest person who can expose Colton’s secret.
NB
Grey Market / RedWire
sections.Nyx Bellweather:
RedWire Handler
“Detective Cross. Cute title. Does Iron still answer?”
Public: Street-level information broker.
Truth: Saw Colton’s talent and recruited him into RedWire. Keeps an Iron Cross file.
Open full dossier
Vibe: Friendly until she is not. Always smiling like she knows the last page.
Secret: Has a sealed file labeled IRON CROSS — DO NOT BURN.
First Appearance: Leaves Colton a message using his old RedWire signal.
DM Use: Pull Colton’s old shadows back into the story.
BS
Grey Market / RedWire
sections.Bell Saint:
RedWire Collector
“This is going to hurt, and I am sorry about the inconvenience.”
Public: None.
Truth: Debt collector, intimidation specialist, and cleanup man.
Open full dossier
Vibe: Polite, stylish, terrifying.
Secret: Was ordered not to kill Colton years ago and does not know why.
First Appearance: Appears after someone fails to pay or talks too much.
DM Use: Street-level antagonist with style and menace.
C
Grey Market / RedWire
sections.Cricket:
Memory Shard Broker
“That’s not a drive. That’s a confession someone tried to make portable.”
Public: Small-time dealer in old tech and illegal data movement.
Truth: Can identify shard containers, seals, makers, and who could afford them.
Open full dossier
Vibe: Nervous, quick, useful in exactly the way that gets people killed.
Secret: Saw a similar container linked to a BCI-adjacent black clinic.
First Appearance: Hiding in a market stall, ready to bolt at the word BCI.
DM Use: Session 1 tech ID and shard lore.
PN
Grey Market / RedWire
sections.Dr. Pax Neme:
Street Surgeon
“Cash first. Sympathy after anesthesia.”
Public: Unlicensed medical fixer.
Truth: Treats wounds, removes trackers, patches cybernetics, and keeps medical events off-record.
Open full dossier
Vibe: Back-alley doctor with surprisingly good bedside manner.
Secret: Once worked in a BCI-approved trauma clinic.
First Appearance: Behind a noodle shop, under humming surgical lights.
DM Use: Safe-ish healing, tracker removal, off-record consequences.
ID
Grey Market / RedWire
sections.Ivo Dent:
Transit Smuggler
“The city has veins. I just know where to cut.”
Public: Maintenance worker, courier, tunnel rat depending on who asks.
Truth: Moves people through monorail maintenance routes and runoff infrastructure.
Open full dossier
Vibe: Fast-talking map goblin with a survival grin.
Secret: Knows a tunnel beneath an abandoned BCI processing site.
First Appearance: Charges the party extra because they look chased.
DM Use: Movement when official routes are unsafe.
MR
Grey Market / RedWire
sections.Mags Rook:
Fence for BCI Gear
“Stolen? No. Rehomed from fascists. Big difference.”
Public: Scrap dealer.
Truth: Sells stolen armor pieces, terminal parts, weapons, and restricted BCI hardware.
Open full dossier
Vibe: Greasy hands, bright eyes, no refund policy.
Secret: Has a damaged BCI Elite helmet with undeleted raid footage.
First Appearance: In a stall made of armored doors and old transit signs.
DM Use: Gear, clues hidden in tech, black-market upgrades.
sections.Session Hooks:
• Nyx contacts Colton with an Iron Cross signal.
• Cricket identifies the broken memory shard container.
• Varo offers help for a favor that sounds harmless and absolutely is not.
• Mags has BCI gear containing footage from a raid nobody admits happened.
GM Secret Board
sections.Faction Secrets:
• RedWire knows more about Colton than Colton wants the party to know.
• Someone ordered Bell Saint not to kill Colton.
• The Grey Market has handled memory shards connected to BCI black clinics.
• Information brokers may sell the party’s investigation to both sides.

sections.Faction Overview:
Public FaceThis is the visible government of Meridian Spire: mayoral speeches, council votes, news conferences, city safety initiatives, and public order campaigns.
Actual TruthCivic leadership provides legitimacy and political cover for BCI authority, Continuance funding, elite protection, and controlled public narratives.
Player-Safe ReadPlayers know these people from screens, speeches, license offices, and public scandals. They are the smiling face over the machinery.
GM WarningPolitical NPCs should rarely threaten directly. They make the world move against the party while sounding reasonable.
TerritoryCivic Core, council chambers, media towers, donor galas, licensing offices, public safety boards.
city sealwhite marble towersblue civic screensgold council pinspress lights
sections.Internal Structure:
Mayor’s OfficePublic leadership, crisis addresses, BCI coordination, city image.
Council BlocLaws, hearings, budgets, oversight theater, opposition pressure.
Public MediaNarrative control, panic management, heroic framing of state power.
Licensing BureauLegal status of investigators, case access, permits, penalties.
Donor ClassCorporate money, research funding, security expansion, elite survival interests.
sections.Mayor Evangeline Cross:
Mayor of Meridian Spire
“Stability is compassion at scale.”
Public: Polished, reform-minded mayor focused on public safety, cognitive health, and order.
Truth: Political bridge between BCI, wealthy families, city government, and Continuance.
Open full dossier
Vibe: Elegant, beloved by people who do not know better.
Secret: May be connected to Colton’s family name or to the systems that erased his parents.
First Appearance: Public broadcasts after BCI incidents or citywide emergency addresses.
DM Use: Political face of the campaign; decides when players become threats.
CN
Civic Power
sections.Councilor Nia Ravel:
Opposition Councilor
“Oversight that cannot say no is just applause.”
Public: Council critic of BCI overreach.
Truth: Potential ally if the party provides evidence strong enough to risk her career.
Open full dossier
Vibe: Brave enough to speak, careful enough to survive.
Secret: Her sibling was memory-edited after a protest.
First Appearance: A hearing clip questioning BCI authority.
DM Use: Political ally with limits and consequences.
MQ
Civic Power
sections.Mara Quell:
BCI-Approved News Anchor
“Remain calm. Verified updates will follow.”
Public: Trusted voice during crisis broadcasts.
Truth: Frames events in ways that support BCI narratives and calm public doubt.
Open full dossier
Vibe: Warm, fake-safe, professionally soothing.
Secret: Received an anonymous Ruiz file and has not opened it yet.
First Appearance: Reports on the apartment incident using language that makes the party sound suspicious.
DM Use: Reputation pressure and possible media crack later.
DO
Civic Power
sections.Dax Orro:
PI License Handler
“I don’t care what you found. I care what you can prove without getting my office raided.”
Public: City licensing contact for PI permits, case permissions, and warnings.
Truth: Knows some cases are cursed and quietly flags them because people vanish around them.
Open full dossier
Vibe: Overworked clerk with survival instincts and a permanent headache.
Secret: Keeps a list of DO NOT TOUCH case numbers that disappear from systems.
First Appearance: Calls the party after Session 1 to warn their licenses are being reviewed.
DM Use: Legal leash, warning siren, reluctant official access.
MA
Civic Power
sections.Minister Alaric Venn:
Minister of Cognitive Safety
“A safe mind is a loyal city.”
Public: Cabinet-level voice for cognitive health and social stability.
Truth: Uses fear to justify surveillance, memory compliance, and BCI budget expansions.
Open full dossier
Vibe: Comforting authoritarian with excellent diction.
Secret: Knows about non-medical memory correction but calls it emergency stabilization.
First Appearance: Appears beside Veyne after an “illegal memory contamination” alert.
DM Use: Give BCI policy a political mouthpiece.
SD
Civic Power
sections.Seris Dane:
Corporate Lobbyist
“Everyone hates corruption until they need funding.”
Public: High-end policy consultant connecting business and government.
Truth: Links Continuance, BCI, wealthy families, and city contracts.
Open full dossier
Vibe: Velvet handshake, razor ledger.
Secret: Maintains private donor channels for continuity research.
First Appearance: At a gala, already knowing which PC should not be there.
DM Use: Connects money trails without looking like a villain.
sections.Session Hooks:
• Mayor Cross frames a BCI crackdown as compassionate stability.
• Nia Ravel asks the party for evidence she can use in a public hearing.
• Dax warns the party that one more incident could cost them their PI licenses.
• Mara Quell receives the Ruiz file and becomes a possible media wild card.
GM Secret Board
sections.Faction Secrets:
• Political leaders know different amounts of the truth, which makes guilt uneven.
• The licensing office can be used as a pressure valve against the party.
• A donor network quietly funds Continuance in exchange for future access.
• Mayor Cross’s surname may become a Colton pressure point.
